subject: maths
name: Real Numbers
intro: Euclid's Division Lemma, Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, HCF/LCM prime factorisation se, aur irrational numbers ka proof — sab is chapter mein hain!

topics.name: Euclid's Division Lemma & HCF
topics.desc: Kisi bhi do positive integers ko divide karke HCF nikalo

steps.title: Euclid Ka Magical Division Trick! 🏛️
steps.body:
Socho Ramesh ke paas 135 chocolates hain aur 5 friends hain. 135 = 5 × 27 + 0. Yahi hai Euclid's Division Lemma — kisi bhi do numbers a aur b ke liye:
a = bq + r  (jahan 0 ≤ r < b)
Aur isi se hum HCF nikaal sakte hain!

steps.title: Euclid's Division Algorithm
steps.body:
**Theorem:** Kisi bhi do positive integers a aur b ke liye, unique integers q aur r exist karte hain:
a = bq + r, jahan 0 ≤ r < b

**HCF Algorithm Steps:**
1. Bade number ko chhote se divide karo
2. Remainder 0 aaye tab tak repeat karo
3. Jab r = 0, uss waqt ka divisor = HCF!

steps.title: Euclid's Division Lemma
steps.note:
a = dividend, b = divisor, q = quotient, r = remainder
HCF Algorithm: Jab tak r ≠ 0, apply karte raho
Jab r = 0, uss waqt ka b = HCF

steps.title: HCF(135, 225) Nikalo
steps.question: HCF(135, 225) Euclid's Division Algorithm se nikalo

questions.question: Kya 6n kabhi 0 se end ho sakta hai?
questions.answer: Nahi! 6n = (2×3)n. Isme 5 ka prime factor nahi. Isliye kabhi nahi.

steps.title: Board Exam Tips
points: Euclid Algorithm mein hamesha bada ÷ chhota karo
Jab remainder = 0 aaye, uss divisor = HCF
HCF × LCM = product of two numbers (SIRF 2 numbers!)

topics.name: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic
topics.desc: Har composite number uniquely prime factors ka product

steps.title: Prime Numbers Ki Factory! 🔢
steps.body:
Ek factory mein sirf prime numbers kaam karte hain — 2, 3, 5, 7...
Yeh factory har koi bhi number bana sakti hai!
12 = 2 × 2 × 3
84 = 2 × 2 × 3 × 7
Aur yeh amazing hai ki yeh ek UNIQUE tarika hai!

steps.title: Theorem
steps.body:
**Theorem:** Har composite number ko primes ke product ke roop mein likha ja sakta hai — UNIQUELY!

**HCF:** Common primes ki SABSE CHHOTI powers
**LCM:** Sab primes ki SABSE BADI powers

**Important:** HCF × LCM = a × b (SIRF 2 numbers ke liye!)

steps.title: Yaad Rakho!
points: HCF = smallest power of COMMON primes
LCM = LARGEST power of ALL primes
HCF × LCM = a × b (sirf 2 numbers ke liye)
4ⁿ = 2²ⁿ — koi 5 nahi, isliye 0 se end nahi hoga!

steps.title: Mathematician Ki Khoj! 🔬
steps.body:
Ek mathematician ne prove kiya: √2 ko fraction mein nahi likha ja sakta!
Usne try kiya √2 = p/q... aur result mila p aur q dono mein 2 common — CONTRADICTION!
Yahi 'Proof by Contradiction' hai.

steps.title: √2 Irrational — Complete Proof
steps.note:
1. Maan lo √2 = p/q (coprime, q≠0)
2. p² = 2q² → 2 divides p → p=2m
3. 4m² = 2q² → q²=2m² → 2 divides q
4. 2 divides BOTH p aur q — CONTRADICTION!
5. ∴ √2 is IRRATIONAL ✅

questions.question: HCF(6, 20) prime factorisation se kya hoga?
questions.explanation: 6 = 2¹×3¹ aur 20 = 2²×5¹. Common prime = 2. Smallest power = 2¹. HCF = 2.

questions.question: LCM(6, 20) kya hai?
questions.hint: LCM = sabse badi powers of ALL primes = 2² × 3 × 5

questions.question: √2 ke baare mein kya sach hai?
questions.hint: Proof by contradiction — fraction form mein nahi likha ja sakta.
questions.explanation: √2 = 1.41421356... kabhi khatam nahi hota. Proof by contradiction se irrational sabit hota hai.

questions.question: 4ⁿ ka last digit 0 kab hoga?
questions.explanation: 0 se end hone ke liye 10=2×5 ka factor chahiye. 4ⁿ=2²ⁿ mein sirf 2 hai. Isliye kabhi nahi.

questions.question: 5 - √3 kya hai?
questions.hint: Rational - Irrational = Irrational hota hai hamesha.
questions.explanation: Maan lo rational. Toh √3 = 5 - (p/q) = rational. Par √3 irrational — contradiction!

questions.question: HCF(a,b)=18 aur LCM(a,b)=108. a=36 hai toh b?
3√2 kya hai?
questions.explanation: Maan lo 3√2 = p/q → √2 = p/3q = rational. Par √2 irrational — contradiction!

questions.question: Sonia 18 min mein ek round, Ravi 12 min mein. Kitne min baad milenge?
HCF(306, 657) = 9 hai toh LCM kya hai?
6ⁿ kisi bhi n ke liye 0 se end ho sakta hai?
questions.explanation: 0 se end ke liye 2 aur 5 dono chahiye. 6ⁿ=2ⁿ×3ⁿ mein 5 nahi. Isliye kabhi nahi.

questions.question: 1/√2 kya hai?
questions.explanation: Maan lo 1/√2=p/q. Toh √2=q/p=rational. Par √2 irrational — contradiction!

questions.question: HCF(a,b) = 1 ka matlab kya hai?
questions.explanation: HCF=1 → a aur b mein koi common factor nahi. Inhe coprime ya relatively prime kehte hain.

questions.question: Prove karo ki n²-n hamesha even hai. Reason?
questions.hint: n²-n = n(n-1). Consecutive integers mein ek hamesha even!
questions.explanation: n(n-1) = consecutive integers. Inme se ek hamesha even. Even×anything = even. ✅
